key_items: Crypto markets shed $80B after fresh US strikes on Iran amid geopolitical tensions
Bitcoin funding spike shows longs defending $70K with critical support levels being tested
Bitcoin just absorbed a single $1.3B IBIT block trade with barely any price movement showing institutional accumulation
reasoning: Near-term technicals show BTC struggling to hold key support levels ($70K-$75K) amid geopolitical uncertainty and miners pivoting to AI, creating downward pressure. However, significant institutional buying (large block trades absorbed with minimal slippage, Kraken vault deposits, strategic accumulation exceeding daily mining supply) and bullish long-term narratives (Cathie Wood's $750K-$1.25M targets, major financial players securing licenses) suggest underlying support that could stabilize or reverse losses in the 1-7 day window.
risks: Further geopolitical escalation between US and Iran combined with regulatory headwinds (CFTC challenges, stalled pro-crypto legislation) could trigger a breakdown below $70K support and cascade liquidations in leveraged longs.
